creator: egypcio@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Andrey Korobkov from comment #0)

Hi Andrey,

sadly I need to close this one based on the fact that we do not have an official 3.14 Release for the OONI Probe CLI yet - only an Alpha release is available.

that translates to: should one need to get 3.14.0-alpha [1], it must be than built from scratch or with a modified version of this port - but that modification can not be part of our official tree. sorry.

we are currently tracking the latest stable release [2], and it will stay like this til upstream releases an official update to that.

knowing that this is a pretty important tool to detect and test stuff against online censorship - specially after recent events [3] - I can try to help you out to get this latest alpha release running (assuming you have git and go installed on your system):

  $ git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch v3.14.0-alpha https://github.com/ooni/probe-cli
  $ cd probe-cli
  $ go build -v -tags=ooni,nomk -ldflags '-s -w' -v ./cmd/ooniprobe

by doing the steps documented above you get a binary on the current directory named 'ooniprobe' and you can use it right away :) if you prefer to move it be used via PATH env do the following (assuming you have sudo rights):

  $ sudo mv ooniprobe /usr/local/bin/.

the previously documented recommendations are to be followed "as is" at your own.

[1] https://github.com/ooni/probe-cli/releases/tag/v3.14.0-alpha
[2] https://github.com/ooni/probe-cli/releases/latest
[3] https://blog.torproject.org/tor-censorship-in-russia/
